steps.question: What have you been dealing with?
What have you tried so far?
How long has this been going on?
Where are things at right now?
Do you feel like you can figure this out on your own?

report:
You've been grinding at this for three to five years, cycling through supplements, diets, prescriptions, and hours of your own research, and the honest summary is "up and down, nothing lasting." That pattern isn't random. The weight that won't budge, the persistent pain, and the mood issues you're fighting are not three separate problems requiring three separate fixes. They share a common root: when your body's ability to produce and use energy breaks down, inflammation rises, hormones shift, gut function deteriorates, and your metabolism slows to protect itself. The weight becomes almost impossible to lose because your body is actively defending a stressed state. The pain persists because inflammation has nowhere to go when the systems that resolve it are underpowered. The mood suffers because the same energy crisis starving your cells is starving your brain. Everything you've tried has probably touched one piece of this at a time, which is why you get temporary improvements that fade. The trajectory here is not neutral. Three to five years in, these patterns tend to deepen, not plateau. The weight creeps, the pain becomes more entrenched, and the emotional toll of trying and failing compounds on itself.

You've clearly got the drive to fix this, and you're not short on effort. But the reason nothing has lasted is that effort alone can't untangle a system this interconnected. Stacking another supplement on top of what you've already tried, or cycling through another diet, will land you right back in the same "up and down" loop because none of those approaches address why your body is stuck in the first place. The weight, the pain, and the mood are all downstream of the same broken systems, and they need to be addressed together, at the root, by people who understand how metabolism, gut health, inflammation, and hormones actually interact. Continuing to piece this together on your own means more years of the same cycle, and honestly, a body that becomes harder to course-correct with each passing year.

The next page will show you exactly how we work with people in your situation and what the path forward looks like.
